layout.title: Da Nanni – Neapolitan Pizzeria and Italian Restaurant in Barcelona
layout.description: Neapolitan pizza made with long-fermented dough and Italian trattoria cooking across six locations in Barcelona. A Neapolitan family business since 2018.

home.title: Neapolitan Pizzeria and Italian Restaurant in Barcelona
home.description: Neapolitan pizzeria and Italian restaurant in Barcelona. Pizza made with 48-hour fermented dough and baked in a wood-fired oven at six locations, from the Born to Gràcia, also available for takeaway.

paraLlevar.title: Takeaway pizza in Barcelona
paraLlevar.subtitle: ‘Pocket-sized’ Neapolitan pizza in 24 and 33 cm sizes, fresh from the oven.
paraLlevar.intro: Two spots in the Gòtic and the Raval, designed so you can order and carry on strolling.
paraLlevar.domicilioHint: Would you rather we brought it to you?
paraLlevar.metaTitle: Takeaway Pizza in Barcelona – Neapolitan from €3
paraLlevar.metaDescription: ‘Pocket-sized’ Neapolitan pizza from €3 in the 24 cm size and from €6 in the 33 cm, ready in minutes. Da Nanni in the Gothic Quarter and the Raval, every day from 12:00 to 22:30.
paraLlevar.promoText: In the evening we carry on with the large 33 cm size until 22:30.
paraLlevar.articleTitle: Pizza by the slice, Naples style
paraLlevar.articleP1: In Naples, pizza is also eaten on the street, folded into quarters. That’s the idea behind our two takeaway spots: Neapolitan pizza by the slice in two sizes, 24 cm (the ‘pocket pizza’, available until 16:00) and 33 cm, fresh from the oven and ready in minutes, open all day from 12:00 to 22:30, every day.
paraLlevar.articleP2: The <goticLink>Gothic Quarter</goticLink> spot is at Carrer de la Llibreteria, 10, a stone’s throw from Barcelona Cathedral: it was the first Da Nanni and is still the perfect stop among the narrow streets. The <ravalLink>Raval</ravalLink> spot (Carrer dels Tallers, 72, just steps from the MACBA) adds a bar and a small terrace so you can eat it right there.
paraLlevar.articleP3: Both share the same menu: 24 Neapolitan pizzas, from the classic Margherita to the Tartufata, with vegetarian and vegan options. You can browse it, with photos and videos of every pizza, on the <goticCartaLink>Gòtic menu</goticCartaLink> or the <ravalCartaLink>Raval menu</ravalCartaLink>.

faq.q1: How much does a takeaway pizza cost at Da Nanni?
faq.a1: The 24 cm pocket size starts at €3 (the Marinara; the Margherita is €3.50) and is available until 16:00. The large 33 cm size goes from €6 to €13 and is served all day.
faq.q2: What time is the last order for takeaway pizza?
faq.a2: Every day from 12:00 to 22:30, all day without a break, at both the Gothic Quarter spot (Llibreteria 10) and the Raval spot (Tallers 72).
faq.a3: They’re designed for takeaway: the Raval spot has a bar and a small terrace, with no table service. If you’d rather sit down and enjoy full service, we have four trattorias in the Born, Tallers, Poblenou and Gràcia.
faq.a4: The Gòtic spot delivers through the delivery apps; the Raval spot is collection only for now. The trattorias in the Born, Poblenou and Gràcia deliver too — you’ll find every option on the Delivery page.

domicilio.title: Neapolitan pizza delivery in Barcelona
domicilio.subtitle: From the wood-fired oven to your door: order on Glovo or Just Eat from our branches in the Gòtic, Born, Poblenou and Gràcia.
domicilio.intro: Each branch delivers within its own area. Choose the one nearest you and order straight from its Glovo or Just Eat store.
domicilio.mapaTitle: Check the map to see which one is closest to you
domicilio.mapaText: Turn on your location and we’ll tell you which Da Nanni is closest to home, with buttons to order straight from that branch.
domicilio.recogidaTitle: Would you rather collect it yourself?
domicilio.recogidaText: At our Gòtic and Raval spots we’ll make your pizza fresh for you to take away, and at the trattorias there’s always a table waiting for you.
domicilio.metaTitle: Pizza Delivery in Barcelona – Order on Glovo or Just Eat
domicilio.metaDescription: Wood-fired Neapolitan pizza delivered in Barcelona. Order on Glovo or Just Eat from our branches in the Gòtic, Born, Poblenou and Gràcia.

historia.metaTitle: Our Story – A Journey from Naples to Barcelona
historia.metaDescription: Da Nanni was founded in 2018 by a Neapolitan family in Barcelona. Discover the story behind our siren Partenope, our mascot Nanni and every restaurant we’ve opened.
historia.title: A journey from Naples to Barcelona, one bite at a time
historia.intro1: In 2018, in the vibrant heart of Barcelona, Da Nanni was born. Not just a restaurant, but a promise of love born from the meeting of two extraordinary cultures. The Catalan capital welcomed us with open arms, becoming our second home: a generous land that we decided to repay by bringing with us our most precious treasure, the culinary tradition of Naples.
historia.intro2: The logo features Partenope, the siren who founded Naples. The name Nanni honours the youngest of the family — you’ll spot him here and there, slice of pizza in his mouth — and that spirit runs through every branch, in the music and in the chatter among our team.
historia.heroAlt: Nanni, the youngest of the family, eating a slice of pizza
historia.heroFigcaption: Nanni, the youngest of the family

m1.copy: Barcelona’s first pocket-sized pizza: takeaway pizza by the slice at La Llibreteria, next to the Cathedral.

m2.copy: A second takeaway spot, with a bar and terrace just steps from the MACBA, right in the heart of the Raval.

m3.copy: A trattoria with a terrace on the Rambla del Poblenou – the Da Nanni closest to the sea.

m4.copy: Wood-fired oven and table service next to the Basilica of Santa Maria del Mar, in the Born.

m5.copy: A trattoria with table service, right opposite our takeaway spot in the Raval.

m6.copy: The group’s most comprehensive menu, right on Carrer de Verdi in Gràcia.

historia.filosofiaText: Our secret? No compromises. To us, Neapolitan cuisine is sacred. For this reason, every single key ingredient —from sun-kissed San Marzano tomatoes to DOP Campania buffalo mozzarella, and the flour for our dough— travels directly from Campania to Barcelona. We respect our raw ingredients so as never to alter the genuine flavours of our childhood, guaranteeing the authenticity of recipes passed down through generations.
historia.manifiestoTitle: Our manifesto: pizza, pasta and hospitality
historia.manifiestoIntro: At Da Nanni, the experience doesn't stop at the plate. What has set us apart since 2018 is an unbreakable triad:
historia.pilarPizzaText: Stretched by hand, with a light, airy crust, baked exactly as tradition dictates.
historia.pilarPastaText: Bronze-die extruded, tossed with slow-cooked, flavourful sauces that smell like home.
historia.pilarHospitalidadText: That spontaneous and generous warmth that makes everyone feel like a guest of honour, not just a customer.
historia.viajeText: Stepping through the doors of Da Nanni means closing your eyes in Barcelona and opening them in front of the Gulf of Naples. We want yours to be a true tastebud journey: a sensory experience where the aroma of fresh basil, the warmth of our welcome and the authenticity of the flavours transport you directly to the alleys of Naples.
historia.bienvenida: Welcome to our land. Welcome to Da Nanni.
historia.sirenaAlt: Fontana della Sirena Partenope, in Naples
historia.sirenaFigcaption: Fontana della Sirena Partenope · Piazza Sannazaro, Naples

avisoLegal.p1: In compliance with the duty to provide information set out in Article 10 of Law 34/2002 of 11 July on Information Society Services and Electronic Commerce, users are hereby informed of the following details: the owner of this website is Nanni 2015, S.L., with tax identification number (CIF) B01732486 and registered office at Calle Tuset 8, 08006 Barcelona.
avisoLegal.p2: If you have any queries regarding this legal notice, please contact us by telephone at any of our branches; the numbers are available on the <contacto>contact</contacto> page.

cookies.p1: This site uses technical cookies that are necessary for it to function and, only if you accept them in the cookie notice, third-party analytics cookies: Microsoft Clarity (heatmaps and anonymous browsing recordings) and Google Analytics (visitor statistics). If you reject them, the website still works as normal and no analytics cookies are installed.
cookies.p2: You can configure your browser to block or delete cookies stored on your device at any time.

privacidad.p1: Data controller: Nanni 2015, S.L., with CIF B01732486 and registered office at Calle Tuset 8, 08006 Barcelona.
privacidad.p2: Any personal data you provide via the contact or booking form will be used exclusively to process your request (table booking, takeaway order or general enquiry) and will not be passed on to third parties unless required by law.
privacidad.p3: You may exercise your rights of access, rectification, erasure and objection by writing to danannipoblenou@gmail.com, by post to the registered office address, or by calling any of our branches.

mejorPizzeria.metaTitle: Best Pizzeria in Barcelona – According to {count} Reviews
mejorPizzeria.metaDescription: Looking for the best pizzeria in Barcelona? The six Da Nanni Neapolitan pizzerias have {count} Google reviews between them, with an average of {rating}★. From the Gòtic to Gràcia: pizza by the slice from €3, or a trattoria with a wood-fired oven.
mejorPizzeria.h1: The best pizzeria in Barcelona? The reviews say so
mejorPizzeria.intro: We’re not going to tell you we’re the best pizzeria in Barcelona: that’s for the people who eat here every day to decide. Six Neapolitan locations, from the Gòtic to Gràcia, and thousands of public Google reviews you can check before you come.
mejorPizzeria.numerosText: Public ratings from each branch’s Google listing (July 2026). No tricks: you’ll also see which one has the lowest score.
mejorPizzeria.quotesTitle: What they say, word for word
mejorPizzeria.quotesText: Direct quotes from Google reviews, verifiable on each branch’s listing.
mejorPizzeria.occasionsTitle: The best one, depending on what you’re after
mejorPizzeria.occasionsIntro: “The best” depends on the plan: a quiet dinner isn’t the same as a slice of pizza between museums.

occasions.x1: Da Nanni Tallers, 4.7★: a full-service Neapolitan trattoria at Carrer dels Tallers 69, next to Plaça de Catalunya.
occasions.x2: The first Da Nanni, next to the Cathedral: pizza by the slice in 24 and 33 cm sizes from €3, and the most-reviewed branch in the group.
occasions.x3: High ceilings, a wood-fired oven and the historic atmosphere of one of the neighbourhood’s most characterful streets, a stone’s throw from Santa Maria del Mar.
occasions.x4: On the Rambla del Poblenou, the Da Nanni closest to the sea: open all day from midday to midnight, with a terrace.
occasions.x5: On Carrer de Verdi, Gràcia: pizza, antipasti, fresh pasta and a select Italian wine list.
occasions.t6: A quick stop in the Raval
occasions.x6: Pocket pizza next to the MACBA, with a bar and a small terrace so you don’t miss a beat of the day.

mejorPizzeria.occasionsOutro: Want to know what makes a real Neapolitan pizza different — the dough, the San Marzano tomatoes, the wood-fired oven? We tell all on <napolitana>our Neapolitan pizza page</napolitana>, or head straight to <cartas>the menus</cartas>, with photos and videos of every dish.

faq.q1: What is the best pizzeria in Barcelona?
faq.a1: According to Google ratings, the highest-scoring of our six branches is {name}, with {topRating} stars. Between them, the six Da Nanni pizzerias have more than {count} reviews, with an average rating of {avgRating}.
faq.q2: How much does it cost to eat at Da Nanni?
faq.a2: From €3 for a Marinara by the slice at the takeaway spots in the Gòtic and the Raval. In the large 33 cm size, pizzas go from €6 to €13, and at the trattorias you can add antipasti, fresh pasta and Italian wine.
faq.q3: Do I need to book a table?
faq.a3: At the four trattorias (Born, Tallers, Poblenou and Gràcia) you can book by phone. The two takeaway spots in the Gòtic and the Raval don’t take bookings: just order and your pizza is ready in minutes.
faq.a4: Yes. The branches in the Gòtic, Born, Poblenou and Gràcia deliver via Glovo and Just Eat.

pizzeriaNapolitana.metaDescription: Authentic Neapolitan pizza in Barcelona: long-fermented dough, wood-fired oven and ingredients from Campania. Six Da Nanni pizzerias from the Gòtic to Gràcia, for dining in, takeaway or home delivery.
pizzeriaNapolitana.title: Neapolitan pizzeria in Barcelona
pizzeriaNapolitana.intro: We’re a family from Naples with six pizzerias in Barcelona. The same traditional Neapolitan pizza as always — long-fermented dough and ingredients brought directly from Campania — to enjoy at the table, take away as you stroll, or have delivered to your door.
pizzeriaNapolitana.section1Intro: For us, Neapolitan cooking is sacred: we make no compromises. Every pizza is hand-stretched, with a light, airy crust, and baked the way tradition demands.
pizzeriaNapolitana.card1Text: Long fermentation for a light, easily digestible dough, hand-stretched to order in every branch.
pizzeriaNapolitana.card2Text: San Marzano tomatoes, Campania DOP buffalo mozzarella and flour that travel directly from Campania to Barcelona.
pizzeriaNapolitana.card3Text: Wood-fired ovens in our trattorias and freshly made pizza by the slice at our takeaway spots.
pizzeriaNapolitana.section2Title: Six pizzerias from the Gòtic to Gràcia
pizzeriaNapolitana.section2Intro: Four trattorias with table service in the Born, the Raval, Poblenou and Gràcia, and two takeaway pizza spots in the Gothic Quarter and the Raval. Choose whichever is closest to you.
pizzeriaNapolitana.section3Title: How would you like it today?
pizzeriaNapolitana.howMesaText: Four Italian restaurants with wood-fired ovens, fresh pasta and Italian wines. Book by phone.
pizzeriaNapolitana.howLlevarText: ‘Pocket-sized’ Neapolitan pizza by the slice, in 24 and 33 cm sizes, ready in minutes in the Gòtic and the Raval.
pizzeriaNapolitana.howDomicilioText: Straight from the oven to your door via Glovo or Just Eat from the Gòtic, Born, Poblenou and Gràcia.
pizzeriaNapolitana.exploreText: Fancy seeing what comes out of our kitchens before you decide? Take a look at <carta>the menu</carta>, with photos and videos of every dish, or discover <historia>our story</historia>: that of a Neapolitan family who started out in 2018 selling pizza by the slice next to the Cathedral and today have trattorias all over Barcelona. Still not sure? See <resenas>what thousands of Google reviews say</resenas> about our restaurants.

faq.a1: The long-fermented, hand-stretched dough, the light, airy crust, and ingredients from Campania: San Marzano tomatoes, DOP buffalo mozzarella and olive oil. In our trattorias, the pizzas are baked in a wood-fired oven, the way tradition demands.
faq.q2: Where is Da Nanni in Barcelona?
faq.a2: We have six locations: two takeaway spots in the Gothic Quarter (Llibreteria 10) and the Raval (Tallers 72), and four trattorias with table service in the Born (Rec 30), Tallers 69, Rambla del Poblenou 20 and Gràcia (Verdi 35).
faq.a3: Yes — you can book by phone at all four trattorias. The takeaway spots don’t take bookings: just order, and in a few minutes your freshly baked pizza is ready.
faq.a4: Yes. The branches in the Gòtic, Born, Poblenou and Gràcia deliver via Glovo and Just Eat.

cookieBanner.text: We use analytics cookies (Microsoft Clarity and Google Analytics) to understand how the website is used and to improve it. They are only activated if you accept them.
